verdict_reasoning: KLK2 (kallikrein-2) is a secreted serine protease. It is synthesized as a preproenzyme, signal peptide directs it through the secretory pathway, and the mature protein is released extracellularly into the fluid/tissue environment (notably seminal plasma). It has no transmembrane domain, no GPI anchor, and no documented stable wash-resistant attachment to a cell-surface protein. Its extracellular presence is as a soluble secreted enzyme, not as a stably membrane-anchored protein. Therefore, the protein body is not surface accessible in the sense required for membrane-targeted modalities.
